  "findings": [
    "Isolated metal parts are not properly bonded to the electrical grounding system, creating a shock and electrocution hazard."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work in the immediate area of the unbonded equipment.",
    "Install temporary or permanent bonding jumpers to connect all isolated metal parts to the equipment grounding conductor (EGC) or grounding electrode system."
  ],
  "rationale": "Isolated metal parts that are likely to become energized must be bonded to the grounding system to ensure a low-impedance path for fault current, which facilitates the operation of overcurrent protective devices. Leaving them isolated violates NEC requirements and poses a severe shock hazard.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 250.104",
    "NEC 250.30"
  ]
}
